title: Browser Origins, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Fields · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A server pairing session predicts request, handler, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before building the first browser request trust and cross-origin policy for browser origins, cors, csrf, cookies, and security fields. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of browser request trust and cross-origin policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish origin, site, same-origin policy, CORS read permission, preflight, credentials, and server authorization.
Validate exact origins, methods, requested headers, credential mode, exposed headers, max age, Vary, null origin, and rejection behavior.
Use safe-method invariants, Go CrossOriginProtection, Sec-Fetch-Site and Origin policy, tokens when needed, and narrowly reviewed bypasses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Origin Site Cors Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 3159 in a recovery rehearsal with an ambiguous database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to keep CORS response sharing separate from CSRF mutation authorization; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish origin, site, same-origin policy, CORS read permission, preflight, credentials, and server authorization.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Origin Site Cors Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish origin, site, same-origin policy, cors read permission, preflight, credentials, and server authorization. For Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Origin Site Cors Boundary, keep Origin Site Cors Boundary connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
